title: Cover read_protocol_message framing error paths and write_json_result with tests
description:
The MCP message framing reader `read_protocol_message` has three error/robustness
paths with no test coverage:

- missing Content-Length header -> McpCliError::Protocol("missing Content-Length ...")
- invalid Content-Length value (non-numeric) -> Protocol("invalid Content-Length ...")
- unexpected EOF after headers began but before a blank line -> Protocol("unexpected EOF ...")

Additionally the owned `write_json_result` helper (as opposed to the already-used
`write_json_result_ref`) has no direct test.

These guard the JSON-RPC stdio transport against malformed client framing producing
panics instead of clean structured Protocol errors, and lock the owned envelope
writer's output shape.

Scope: test-only additions in src/lib.rs #[cfg(test)]. The framing reader is a
private fn, so tests live in the same module (already the case). No production code
changes expected. Keep generic.

Filed by mcp-cli-dev-aur-0 while progressing the lane.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Cover MCP ping, initialized-notification, and unknown-method (-32601) paths with tests
description:
The stdio MCP server (`McpServer::handle_request`) implements several JSON-RPC
behaviors that currently have no test coverage:

- `ping` -> returns `{"result": {}}` with the request id.
- `notifications/initialized` -> returns no response (notification; id-less).
- unknown/unsupported method -> JSON-RPC error object with code `-32601` and an
  "unsupported MCP method" message.

Only initialize/tools.list/tools.call are exercised today (via
stdio_server_handles_initialize_list_and_call). Adding focused tests for ping,
the initialized notification (no reply), and the unknown-method error path locks
down protocol correctness and guards against regressions in the JSON-RPC framing
layer.

Scope: test-only additions in src/lib.rs #[cfg(test)] using the existing
frame_request / parse_framed_responses helpers. No production code changes
expected. Keep generic per crate constraints.

Filed by mcp-cli-dev-aur-0 while progressing the lane (operator: workers improve
projects).
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: MCP initialize should negotiate protocolVersion, not hardcode 2024-11-05
description:
Observed while implementing bd-909a82. In `McpServer::handle_request`, the `initialize` response hardcodes `"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05"` and ignores the `protocolVersion` the client sent in the initialize params. Per the MCP spec the server should echo back the client's requested protocol version when it can support it, and otherwise respond with a version it does support so the client can decide whether to proceed or disconnect. Hardcoding the oldest revision pins every session to 2024-11-05 even when a modern client (e.g. 2025-03-26 / 2025-06-18) connects; most clients will silently downgrade, but it is suboptimal and not spec-aligned negotiation.

Suggested fix: read the client's requested `protocolVersion` from initialize params, and respond with that value if it is in a small set of supported versions, otherwise fall back to the server's latest supported version (currently 2024-11-05). Keep the crate generic — supported-version set can be a const slice. Add a test asserting a known client version is echoed and an unknown one falls back.

File: src/lib.rs, `initialize` arm of `handle_request`. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-909a82 session.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: MCP stdio server should return JSON-RPC errors for invalid request / invalid params, not tear down the session
description:
A long-running MCP stdio server (McpServer::serve_transport) must keep serving across many requests on one connection. Today only `-32601 method not found` is handled gracefully; two other malformed-but-parseable cases instead propagate a Rust error out of `serve_transport`, terminating the entire session and dropping all subsequent valid requests:

1. A JSON value that parses but is not a valid JSON-RPC request (e.g. missing `method`): `handle_request_value` does `serde_json::from_value::<JsonRpcRequest>(request)?` which propagates. Per JSON-RPC 2.0 this should be `-32600 Invalid Request` (id recovered from the raw value if present, else null) and the server should keep running.
2. A `tools/call` with malformed `params` (e.g. missing `name`): `handle_request` does `serde_json::from_value::<ToolCallParams>(...)?` which propagates. Per JSON-RPC 2.0 this should be `-32602 Invalid params` with the request id, and the server should keep running.

Fix: handle both cases inside the request dispatch, returning the appropriate JSON-RPC error response object and continuing the serve loop. Add regression tests proving a session survives a bad message and still answers a following valid request.

Explicit non-goal (respecting bd-c7aeca): the non-JSON transport line case (`serde_json::from_slice` in `serve_transport`, exercised by `stdio_server_rejects_non_json_input_instead_of_hanging`) was deliberately made to fail visibly rather than hang. This bead does not change that interactive-garbage behavior; it only fixes the parseable-JSON cases. Whether non-JSON lines should also become `-32700` + continue is left as a separate operator decision.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Cover JsonEnvelope round-trip deserialize and initialize handshake completeness
description:
Two public-contract behaviors lack test coverage:

1. JsonEnvelope<T> round-trip deserialize. The envelope is a #[serde(tag="status")]
   tagged enum deriving Deserialize and is the crate's primary --json wire contract
   that consumers parse. Today only the serialize direction is tested; nothing proves
   the success and error variants deserialize back from their serialized JSON form
   (status tag dispatch, data/error payloads, meta).

2. initialize response completeness. The stdio initialize handler emits
   protocolVersion ("2024-11-05"), capabilities.tools.listChanged (false), and
   serverInfo.version (from StdioServerConfig.server_version), but the existing test
   only asserts serverInfo.name. A small assertion set locks the handshake shape.

Scope: test-only additions in src/lib.rs #[cfg(test)]. No production changes. Keep
generic. Locks the public CLI envelope contract bidirectionally and the MCP
initialize handshake.

Filed by mcp-cli-dev-aur-0 while progressing the lane.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: stdio transport must use newline-delimited JSON (MCP), not Content-Length framing
description:
mcp-cli's stdio transport uses LSP-style Content-Length header framing
(read_protocol_message / write_protocol_message), but the MCP stdio transport
specification uses newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON): each JSON-RPC message is a
single line terminated by \n, with no framing headers and no embedded newlines.

Impact: any real MCP client (e.g. `tendril mcp` / `tendril mcp stdio`) sends an
initialize request as a single newline-terminated JSON line with NO
Content-Length header. The server's read loop treats that JSON as a header line,
never finds the expected Content-Length + blank-line terminator, and blocks
reading more "headers" — so it never parses the request and never responds. The
client hangs in "connecting" forever. Operator reports all mcp-cli apps get
stuck connecting under tendril mcp.

Root cause masked by tests: every test (frame_request / parse_framed_responses)
speaks the server's own Content-Length dialect, so all 19 pass while real MCP
clients fail.

Fix: replace Content-Length framing with newline-delimited JSON on both read and
write paths:
- read: read one line, trim trailing \r\n, skip blank lines, parse the line as a
  full JSON-RPC message; EOF -> None.
- write: serialize compact JSON (no embedded newlines) + a single trailing \n,
  flush.
- Update test helpers and assertions to NDJSON. Keep the public serve_stdio /
  serve_transport signatures stable.

Scope: src/lib.rs production framing + tests. P1-ish correctness bug but filed as
task P2.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Cover stdio tools/call error path (isError=true) end-to-end
description:
The stdio tools/call handler returns isError=true with the structured error
envelope embedded in structuredContent when a tool call fails, but only the
success path (isError=false) is tested today
(stdio_server_handles_initialize_list_and_call asserts isError==false).

The error half of the most important MCP operation is uncovered end-to-end
through the framing layer:

- tools/call for an unknown tool name -> isError=true, structuredContent.status
  == "error", error.code == "unknown_tool" (router emits "unknown tool `name`").
- tools/call with arguments that fail typed validation -> isError=true,
  structuredContent carries the validation error category/code.

A regression here would silently stop surfacing errors to MCP clients. Adding a
focused stdio test locks isError propagation and the embedded error envelope shape.

Scope: test-only additions in src/lib.rs #[cfg(test)] using existing
frame_request/parse_framed_responses helpers and build_math_router. No production
changes. Keep generic.

Filed by mcp-cli-dev-aur-0 while progressing the lane.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: McpCliError::category() always returns SerializationError for every variant
description:
`McpCliError::category()` (src/lib.rs) unconditionally returns `ErrorCategory::SerializationError` for every variant, including `Io(io::Error)` and `Protocol(String)`. Any consumer that projects a transport/CLI error into a JsonError via this category therefore mislabels I/O failures and protocol violations as serialization errors.

Fix: match on the variant and map to an appropriate category:
- `Io(_)` -> `ExecutionFailure` (transport I/O failure)
- `Json(_)` -> `SerializationError`
- `Protocol(_)` -> `Validation` (protocol/message violated expectations)

Keep the method a `const fn` (match is const-fn-legal). Add a unit test asserting each variant maps to its category.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Add runnable doctest for README minimal pattern (0 doctests today)
description:
The README "Minimal pattern" section shows a `rust` fenced code block demonstrating
ToolRouter + McpServer usage, but it is not compiled or run as a doctest. `cargo test
--workspace --all-features` reports `Doc-tests mcp_cli ... 0 tests`, so the public
example is never verified against the real API and can silently rot as the surface
evolves.

Suggested fix shape: add a crate-level `//!` doc comment in `src/lib.rs` containing the
same minimal example as a runnable doctest (mirroring the README), or convert the README
block into a tested example. Keep the example generic per the crate's design constraint.
This gives compile-time protection for the documented entry-point pattern.

Observed during autonomous health-check of mcp-cli (check/test/clippy all green, 7 unit
tests pass, 0 doctests). Filed via reflect-session mixin from mcp-cli-dev-aur-0 idle
session.
creator: aurora:mcp-cli:aurora-mcp-cli-mcp-cli-dev-aur-0

- `Io(_)` -> `ExecutionFailure` (transport I/O failure)

Keep the method a `const fn` (match is const-fn-legal). Add a unit test asserting each variant maps to its category.
